hero.title: BackSight
hero.subtitle: A mobile-friendly computer vision prototype designed to detect prolonged nearby presence and identify repeated reappearance.
hero.description: This project explores how real-time computer vision can support personal safety by monitoring nearby people over time, detecting repeated presence patterns, and issuing alerts when a person remains in the environment for unusually long periods.

sections.title: Why this project matters
sections.text:
In many situations, a person may feel uncomfortable when someone stays nearby for an extended period or repeatedly reappears. This project was created as an early-stage prototype to explore whether computer vision can help detect those patterns automatically.

Rather than identifying who a person is, the system focuses on behavioral presence over time: whether someone stays within range for too long, temporarily leaves, and then appears again in a similar visual form. The goal is to provide an additional layer of situational awareness.

sections.title: What the application does today
sections.text: The current version uses a computer vision pipeline to monitor people in the camera frame over time. Currently, the system can:
bullets: Detect people in the camera’s field of view
Keep track of stay duration nearby
Store lightweight visual features for each person
Use color histogram matching for re-identification (Re-ID)
Trigger alerts based on timing and repeated-presence logic

sections.title: Current Challenges & Limitations
sections.text:
As an early prototype, one main challenge is hardware placement. Relying on a phone camera makes it difficult to monitor the area behind the user naturally.

In addition, re-identification based on color histograms is a lightweight approximation. Similar clothing, lighting changes, and fast motion can affect reliability. This is a proof-of-concept, not a finished safety product.

sections.title: Future Direction
sections.text: Future versions could move beyond the phone camera to a small clip-on wearable camera better suited for continuous monitoring.
bullets: Smart alert strategies combining movement and duration
Robust performance in dynamic outdoor environments

footer.note: This demo is intended for testing and experimentation. Results may vary depending on lighting, occlusion, and device performance.
footer.about: An experimental prototype exploring situational awareness through privacy-aware computer vision.
